description: Only one scalar doublet (Phi) and SU(2)_D gauge bosons; no additional fields (fermions, extra scalars)
required_solution: Add additional fields to test robustness
source_note: Simplifies dynamics but may limit parameter space and physical realism

description: SM-Higgs portal (lambda_HPhi |H|^2 |Phi|^2) and gauge kinetic mixing (epsilon/2 F F') neglected for FOPT dynamics
required_solution: Include portal couplings and study their impact on FOPT and GW signal
source_note: FOPT analysis is for dark sector only; portal effects could modify transition. Makes model secluded.

description: Scalar potential has only quartic term at tree level: V = -lambda (Phi^dagger Phi)^2, classically scale-invariant
required_solution: Consider explicit mass terms or other symmetry-breaking patterns
source_note: Mass scales generated radiatively via CW mechanism. Defines the conformal model class.

description: Accidental SO(3) custodial symmetry of scalar potential protects vector DM stability
required_solution: Explicitly verify custodial symmetry and its implications
source_note: Explicitly unbroken in model definition. May stabilize vector DM candidates.

description: Model does not specify dark matter candidate; literature assumes vector DM from SU(2)_D gauge bosons
required_solution: Explicitly state DM candidate and production mechanism
source_note: Literature (2109.11558) discusses vector DM from non-thermal production

description: Dark sector is in thermal equilibrium with itself
required_solution: Verify thermalization conditions for all parameter space
source_note: Validates use of thermal effective potential

description: Dark sector temperature T_D = T_SM (xi = 1), assumed thermal equilibrium with SM bath
required_solution: Study impact of xi != 1 on cosmology and GW predictions
source_note: Determines g_star for Hubble rate calculation. Fixed at xi=1 without justification of coupling mechanism.

description: Boson loops dominate CW beta function; scalar self-coupling contribution to beta_lambda is subdominant compared to gauge sector
required_solution: Include scalar loop corrections to beta_lambda for completeness
source_note: Simplifies CW relation to beta_lambda = 3*g_D^4/(64*pi^2). Valid for g_D not too small.

description: Phase transition occurs at temperatures T << v (strong supercooling regime)
required_solution: Validate approximations in supercooled regime; consider 3D EFT methods
source_note: Justifies high-T expansion approximation in pipeline. alpha >> 0.5 in PTA-preferred region.

description: SU(2)_D gauge coupling range: g_D in [0.01, 12.56] justified by perturbativity (g_D < 4pi)
required_solution: Extend to non-perturbative regime if needed
source_note: Standard perturbativity bound; upper limit not tested in actual scans.

description: Vacuum expectation value range: v in [100, 1e6] GeV, justified by FOPT temperature range for PTA signal
required_solution: Reconcile with PTA-preferred range v ~ 0.6-1.3 GeV
source_note: Original model range differs from PTA-preferred region by orders of magnitude.

description: FOPT scan used g_D range [0.5, 3.0], narrower than model range [0.01, 12.56]
required_solution: Extend scan to full model range or justify truncation
source_note: Scan range chosen for practical reasons; may miss viable regions at low g_D.

description: FOPT scan used v range [0.01, 10] GeV, much lower than original model range [100, 1e6] GeV
required_solution: Justify discrepancy with original model motivation; validate low-v regime
source_note: Lower v range was necessary to obtain f_peak in PTA frequency band. Changes mass scale significantly.

description: PTArcade used log-uniform priors: log10_g_D in [-0.3010, 0.4771], log10_v_GeV in [-2.0, 1.0]
required_solution: Test sensitivity to prior choice
source_note: Standard choice for Bayesian parameter inference in PTA analysis.

description: High-temperature expansion for thermal effective potential (valid in small field/T regime)
required_solution: Implement 3D EFT (dimensional reduction) for supercooled transitions; validate high-T expansion in strong supercooling regime
source_note: May break down in strong supercooling regime (T_* << v) where alpha >> 0.5. Literature (2312.12413) discusses NLO corrections.

description: Gaussian approximation for false vacuum fraction and percolation condition
required_solution: Use exact percolation criterion or numerical simulations
source_note: Standard approximation in semi-analytic pipelines; accuracy unclear for strong transitions

description: Fixed renormalization scale mu = v (no RG running of couplings)
required_solution: Implement RG running and study scale dependence; compare with literature (2210.07075)
source_note: Literature uses RG-improved effective potential. Scale dependence may affect results.

description: No Daisy resummation for thermal masses
required_solution: Implement Daisy resummation for thermal masses; validate impact on phase transition
source_note: Daisy resummation is particularly important for strongly supercooled transitions (2312.12413).

description: Automatic scalar contribution via CW mechanism (lambda from beta_lambda = 3*g_D^4/(64*pi^2))
required_solution: Verify CW relation matches literature and backend implementation
source_note: CW mechanism is central to conformal model; implementation verified against backend.

description: Bulk flow/dissipative bulk flow (dbf) GW spectrum template assumes specific wall velocity (vw=1.0) and sound speed (cs=1/sqrt(3))
required_solution: Study dependence on wall velocity and sound speed; validate template for alpha >> 0.5
source_note: dbf template from 2511.15687 selected as validated for large alpha values

description: 200 points (50%) of FOPT scan failed due to eternal inflation, primarily at higher g_D or v values
required_solution: Investigate eternal inflation regime; understand physical reason; extend scan methodology
source_note: Large failure rate suggests significant parameter space is inaccessible or physically invalid.

description: Model produces very large alpha values (up to 10^16) in PTA-preferred region due to Coleman-Weinberg mechanism
required_solution: Validate all approximations (high-T expansion, Daisy resummation, template) in alpha >> 0.5 regime
source_note: Strong supercooling regime; many standard approximations may break down. Literature (2312.12413) discusses EFT challenges.

description: Discrepancy with literature (2109.11558): backend gives T_p ~ 0.1 v while literature reports sub-GeV T_* for v ~ 100 GeV
required_solution: Investigate origin of discrepancy; verify backend implementation; reconcile conventions
source_note: May be due to different conventions, model details, or backend approximations.

description: All spectra peak at the lowest PTA frequency bin, suggesting actual peak is below PTA frequency range
required_solution: Extend to lower frequencies or verify spectrum can match within PTA range
source_note: Model may inherently produce GW signals at frequencies below PTA sensitivity.

description: PTA-preferred region has v ~ 0.6-1.3 GeV, much lower than original model range [100, 1e6] GeV
required_solution: Re-evaluate model motivation at low v; check consistency with original literature
source_note: Dark gauge bosons have masses 0.28-0.64 GeV in PTA-preferred region

description: No SM portal couplings; dark sector is secluded, limiting direct production constraints and cosmological connections
required_solution: Study impact of adding portal couplings; assess naturalness of secluded scenario
source_note: Limits applicability of most direct detection constraints; affects DM production mechanisms.

description: All perturbative calculations assume g_D < 4pi ~ 12.56; validity breaks down at stronger couplings
required_solution: Monitor perturbativity; flag non-perturbative regions
source_note: Standard perturbativity bound; scan ranges respect this.

description: High-T expansion valid when T^2 >> m^2; may break down in supercooled regime (T_* << v)
required_solution: Implement 3D EFT methods without high-T expansion; validate in supercooled regime
source_note: Literature (2312.12413, 2210.07075) discusses EFT challenges for supercooled transitions.

description: dbf template validated for alpha >> 0.5; higgsless only valid for alpha <= 0.5
required_solution: Continue using dbf template; validate against simulations for extreme alpha (up to 10^16)
source_note: Template choice appropriate for this model's parameter space.

description: Constraints requiring portal couplings not applicable due to epsilon=0, lambda_HPhi=0
required_solution: Document limitation clearly; consider adding portal couplings for completeness
column13: Major limitation: most direct detection constraints cannot be applied to secluded sector.

description: Particle_content lists gauge boson A with dof=3 without clarifying field components vs degrees of freedom
required_solution: Clarify in documentation (already addressed)
source_note: Minor ambiguity; actual dof correctly handled in field_dependent_masses

description: Field G named 'Goldstone boson' but has m^2 = lambda chi^2 (massive); physically pseudo-Goldstone
required_solution: Rename to pseudo-Goldstone (already addressed in model.md)
source_note: Misleading naming; corrected in model documentation

description: W thermal mass formula assumes same thermal correction for all dof; transverse may differ from longitudinal
required_solution: Investigate mode-dependent thermal masses; validate impact
source_note: Approximation consistent with pipeline and literature (2109.11558, 2210.07075)

description: Scalar loop contributions to beta_lambda omitted; valid for g_D not too small
required_solution: Include scalar loops for completeness; quantify impact
source_note: Verified in literature (2109.11558, 2210.07075) as valid approximation

description: Light scalars (m_rho ~ 36-86 MeV, m_G ~ 18-43 MeV) in PTA-preferred region may affect BBN
required_solution: Compute scalar decay widths and lifetimes; assess BBN impact
source_note: Requires new backend for scalar decay calculations

description: Dark sector particles may contribute to N_eff if reheated above decoupling temperature
required_solution: Compute dark sector thermal history and decoupling temperature
source_note: Requires new backend for thermal history computation

description: Vector DM self-interaction cross section ~ g_D^4 / m_W^2 ~ 10^{-2} to 1 cm^2/g may violate observations
required_solution: Compute dark sector scattering amplitudes; compare with observations
source_note: Requires new backend for dark sector scattering calculations

description: No RG running of couplings; fixed mu = v may introduce scale dependence
required_solution: Implement RG running; study scale dependence; compare with literature (2210.07075)
source_note: Literature uses RG-improved effective potential

description: Strong supercooling regime (alpha >> 0.5, up to 10^16) may affect applicability of multiple approximations
required_solution: Systematic validation of all approximations in supercooled regime; consider 3D EFT
source_note: Core theoretical uncertainty for this model

description: PTA-preferred region produces sub-GeV dark gauge bosons and light scalars
required_solution: Re-evaluate all constraints for sub-GeV mass range; consider new experimental searches
source_note: Makes particles accessible to beam-dump and collider constraints if portal couplings exist

description: dbf template extrapolated to extreme alpha (up to 10^16); validation may not extend to this regime
required_solution: Validate template against simulations at extreme alpha; consider alternative templates
source_note: Template from 2511.15687 validated for large alpha but not for alpha ~ 10^16

description: 50% failure rate due to eternal inflation in FOPT scan
required_solution: Investigate eternal inflation criterion; improve numerical stability
